data.title: Netflix's New Stage: Acquisitions, Live Events and Podcasts
data.description: During the Q4 earnings call, Netflix, Inc. discussed its shift towards becoming a broader entertainment platform. The focus was on the impending acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), which Co-CEO Ted Sarandos called a "strategic accelerant" to their core mission. Stock of NFLX is currently declining. As the deal undergoes regulatory scrutiny, Sarandos expressed optimism, highlighting its pro-consumer, pro-innovation, and pro-growth impact. He also emphasized the complementarity of HBO to Netflix's services. The executives also touched upon their new approach to viewer data amidst the transition to Live Events. For the full story, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Greenberg Traurig advises FICOHSA in $100M future flow securitization program, the first from Honduras.
data.description: A prominent global law firm, Greenberg Traurig, LLP, recently provided advisory services to Banco Financiera Comercial Hondureña, S.A. (FICOHSA), a Honduran bank, regarding the structuring of its merchant voucher receivable (MVR) future flow securitization program. This program included the issuance of its first series. The advisory services were rendered in Miami on January 21, 2026.

data.title: Bitcoin skyrockets to $90K, leading to over $1B in crypto liquidations. Trump's tariff decision influences the market.
data.description: On Wednesday, President Trump announced that he will not implement tariffs in his efforts to acquire Greenland. This decision led to a surge in both Bitcoin and stock prices.

data.title: Markets surge as Trump scraps EU tariffs due to Greenland progress. 10 stocks soar. Trump cancels 10% tariff on EU, citing Greenland deal and NATO cooperation.
data.description: Investor confidence rebounded on Wednesday afternoon as President Donald Trump announced that the planned 10% tariffs on European Union countries have been scrapped, following progress in discussions regarding a Greenland framework with NATO. Trump tweeted on Truth Social that the tariffs, set to go into effect on Feb. 1, will no longer be imposed after a "very productive" meeting with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte. Trump expressed optimism about a potential deal concerning Greenland and the Arctic Region, stating that it could greatly benefit the United States and all NATO Nations. Negotiations are set to continue and expand. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Way.com launches AI software for repair shops to boost operations and profits. AI-powered Repair & Maintenance platform unveiled.
data.description: A new AI-powered Repair & Maintenance platform was recently launched by Way.com (Way) in Fremont, California on January 21, 2026. Way is a top provider of tech-enabled software and services for parking, insurance, washes, and related services. The platform aims to enhance operations in shops, maximizing the number of bays occupied and ultimately increasing shop profits. This development signifies a significant step forward in optimizing efficiency and productivity in the automotive industry.

data.title: Investors in CPNG stock from April 6, 2025, to Dec 16, 2025, can join a class action lawsuit. Contact Robbins LLP.
data.description: In San Diego on January 21, 2026, Robbins LLP issued a reminder to stockholders regarding a class action lawsuit filed for investors who bought or acquired securities of Coupang, Inc. (NYSE: CPNG) between April 6, 2025, and December 16, 2025. Coupang, a company claiming to be a prominent player in the industry, faced legal action due to alleged misconduct during the specified period. The lawsuit encompasses all individuals involved in trading CPNG stocks during this eight-month timeframe.

data.title: Correction made by Carnegie Foundation and ETS regarding Skills Progressions for Collaboration, Communication, and Critical Thinking released on 21-Jan-2026.
data.description: The Carnegie Foundation for the Advancement of Teaching and ETS recently unveiled the initial Skills Progressions for Collaboration, Communication, and Critical Thinking on January 21, 2026. This release marks the first of its kind in partnership with ETS. The progressions provide a framework for assessing and developing key skills essential for success in the modern world. This significant announcement was made via a press release distributed over PR Newswire. This collaboration aims to enhance proficiency in important areas such as collaboration, communication, and critical thinking, setting a precedent for future skill development initiatives.

data.title: Investors who lost money in F5, Inc. (NASDAQ: FFIV) should contact Robbins LLP for info on their rights in class action lawsuit.
data.description: On January 21, 2026, Robbins LLP issued a reminder to stockholders regarding a class action lawsuit for investors who bought or acquired F5, Inc. (NASDAQ: FFIV) securities between October 28, 2024, and October 27, 2025. F5 is a global multicloud application company.

data.title: Is Microsoft's AI Spending Paying Off?
data.description: Microsoft Corp. reported solid earnings, easing investor concerns about the return on its heavy investment in artificial intelligence infrastructure. Azure growth remained strong, reassuring investors despite a slight moderation in growth rate. The market is now focused on Microsoft's ability to efficiently convert its AI lead into earnings power, rather than just winning the AI race. Azure growth is seen as a cash generating platform, with more emphasis on margin contribution over headline growth rates. Microsoft's capital expenditures for AI infrastructure, including data center expansion and specialized chips, continue to raise questions about margins. Despite this, the market appears willing to accept a more mature growth profile as long as Azure maintains steady growth.
